claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess this properly, I'll need to gather some information about your situation and your children.

Let me start with a basic question:

**How many children do you have that you'd like me to assess for Child Benefit eligibility?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! I'd like to check if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have 2 children - Alex who is 17, and Blake who is 18.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that context! I'll help you check the eligibility for both Alex and Blake.

Before I dive into the detailed eligibility criteria, let me ask a few clarifying questions to understand your situation better.

**First, do both Alex and Blake currently live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for confirming that. Now let me read the eligibility criteria to make sure I assess this correctly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, I've reviewed the eligibility criteria. I can see that for children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify for Child Benefit if they stay in approved education or training and are under 20.

I have a couple of important questions:

**For Alex (age 17): Are they currently in approved education or training? For example, are they in school, college, university, or an apprenticeship?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in sixth form doing A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. That's helpful information for Alex.

**Now, for Blake (age 18): Are they currently in any approved education or training? For example, are they in university, college, or an apprenticeship?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment. They finished school and haven't gone on to anything else.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me also confirm a couple of other important details to complete my assessment:

**For Blake: Are they currently working? If so, is it for 24 hours or more per week?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't working at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. I have one final question:

**Are you a British citizen or do you have the right to reside in the UK?** (This includes having settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or being a Crown servant, for example.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and I'm a British citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both children. Let me review the rules carefully.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary of Your Child Benefit Eligibility

Based on the information you've provided, here's the outcome:

✅ **Alex (age 17):** You **are eligible** for Child Benefit
- Alex is in approved education (sixth form A-levels) and is under 20, which meets the eligibility criteria.

❌ **Blake (age 18):** You **are not eligible** for Child Benefit
- Blake is 18 and not in any approved education or training. To qualify for Child Benefit for a child aged 16 or over, they must be under 20 and in approved education or training.

**Note:** If Blake's circumstances change in the future—for example, if they enter approved education or training—you may become eligible for them as long as they remain under 20. If you'd like to apply for Child Benefit for Alex or have any other questions, you can contact the Child Benefit Office.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and is currently in approved education (sixth form doing A-levels). According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you can normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Alex meets both these criteria, so you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 18 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you can only qualify for a child aged 16 or over if they are under 20 and in approved education or training. Blake does not meet the education/training requirement, so you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, who is still in approved education (sixth form A-levels) at age 17. However, you are not eligible for Blake, as they are 18 and no longer in approved education or training. As a British citizen living in the UK with children living in your household, you meet the residency and responsibility requirements, but only Alex meets the age and education criteria necessary for eligibility.
